name: ws02: kernel auth — add gate.check to revoke_token (DiD)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #1
- code: `crates/clawft-kernel/src/auth_service.rs:441` (`revoke_token`)
- prior: cert-critical PARTIAL #6

## Problem / gap

`revoke_token` is the third leg of the auth lifecycle (rotate, request, revoke). The other two legs received `gate.check` guards in commit `a0c54a47`, but `revoke_token` did not, leaving a defense-in-depth gap. Without a governance gate, a compromised principal that bypasses the request/rotate paths can still revoke tokens unchecked.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `gate.check` call inserted at the entry of `revoke_token`, mirroring the `rotate_credential` pattern at `auth_service.rs:337`.
- [ ] Unit test asserts the Deny path returns a governance error.
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh check` + `scripts/build.sh clippy` clean.
- [ ] Audit row #1 removed from `02-kernel-governance.md` task list with WEFT-N reference.

## Dependencies

- blocks: closing the 0.7.0 governance audit
- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- Pattern reference: `auth_service.rs:337` (rotate_credential after `a0c54a47`).
- Severity in the audit is HIGH; treated as 0.7.x release-gate-blocker because the audit explicitly classifies it as Defense-in-Depth.

name: ws02: services-api — re-enable auth middleware on /api/* and /ws
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #10
- code: `crates/clawft-services/src/api/mod.rs:309-315` (auth middleware commented out: `// Enable once the UI has a login flow`)
- prior: sprint-16 security-audit M-1

## Problem / gap

API auth middleware is fully implemented but commented out in the tower stack pending a UI login flow. All `/api/*` and `/ws` endpoints are unauthenticated in shipped binaries. This is a HIGH-severity finding from the sprint-16 security audit and a clear release-gate blocker for a public 0.7.0 cut.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Auth middleware enabled by default in `clawft-services/src/api/mod.rs`.
- [ ] UI login flow lands or a token-bootstrap path is documented.
- [ ] Unauthenticated `/api/*` request returns 401.
- [ ] Integration test for an authenticated round-trip.

## Dependencies

- blocks: 0.7.0 publish (any public-facing daemon)
- blocked-by: UI login flow (or documented bootstrap workaround)

## Notes

- The middleware code already exists; this is a config flip + UI dependency, not new work.

name: ws02: services-api — replace CorsLayer::permissive() with deny-by-default
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #11
- code: `crates/clawft-services/src/api/mod.rs:295` (`CorsLayer::permissive()` default when `cors_origins` is empty)
- prior: sprint-16 security-audit M-2

## Problem / gap

When `cors_origins` is unset, the API ships with `CorsLayer::permissive()`, allowing any origin to call the daemon. This composes badly with the disabled auth middleware (row #10) — even with auth on, a permissive CORS default invites CSRF on cookie/session-bearing flows.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Default CORS layer is deny-by-default (empty allow-list, no `permissive()`).
- [ ] Operators must explicitly set `cors_origins` to enable cross-origin access.
- [ ] Test asserts a non-listed origin gets the right CORS headers (no `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`).
- [ ] Migration note in `docs/handoff.md` for any operator relying on the old default.

## Dependencies

- blocks: 0.7.0 publish
- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- XS effort per audit; concrete drop-in change.

name: ws02: services-api — add tower::limit::RateLimitLayer to /api/* and token endpoints
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #12
- code: `crates/clawft-services/src/api/handlers.rs:133` (TODO `tower::limit::RateLimitLayer`)
- prior: sprint-16 security-audit M-3

## Problem / gap

No rate limiting on `/api/*` — token-issuance endpoints in particular are vulnerable to brute force / abuse. The TODO points at `tower::limit::RateLimitLayer` but the layer was never added.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `tower::limit::RateLimitLayer` (or equivalent) attached to the API tower stack.
- [ ] Stricter limits on token endpoints than on read endpoints.
- [ ] Test confirms 429 after burst threshold.
- [ ] Limits configurable via kernel config.

## Dependencies

- blocks: 0.7.0 publish
- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- Pairs naturally with auth middleware (row #10) and CORS (row #11) work.

name: ws02: services-api — add TokenStore::revoke_token + expired-token cleanup
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #13
- code: `crates/clawft-services/src/api/auth.rs` (no `revoke_token` method on `TokenStore`)
- prior: sprint-16 security-audit M-4

## Problem / gap

`TokenStore` cannot revoke an issued token. There is also no background sweep of expired tokens, so the store grows monotonically. Combined, a leaked token cannot be killed and the store eventually exhausts memory under heavy churn.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `TokenStore::revoke_token(id)` lands; revoked tokens fail validation.
- [ ] Periodic cleanup task removes expired tokens.
- [ ] Tests for revoke + cleanup.
- [ ] CLI / RPC exposes revocation to operators.

## Dependencies

- blocks: 0.7.0 publish (security gap)
- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- Severity MEDIUM in audit but treated as 0.7.x release-gate because the security audit explicitly classifies it user-facing.

name: ws02: chain — add optional idempotency_key to ChainEvent (replay protection)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #14
- prior: sprint-16 security-audit M-5

## Problem / gap

`ChainEvent` has no replay protection — an attacker (or buggy integrator) can resubmit the same event and have it appended again. The audit recommends an optional `idempotency_key` field plus a duplicate check before append.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `ChainEvent` gains optional `idempotency_key` field.
- [ ] `ChainManager::append` checks for duplicate keys and short-circuits with success (or distinct error).
- [ ] Wire format and on-disk RVF bump documented; backward-compatible read path.
- [ ] Tests for new + repeat append.

## Dependencies

- blocks: 0.7.0 security posture
- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- Effort M; touches chain serialization. Coordinate with anchoring work.

name: ws02: tooling — add cargo audit to scripts/build.sh gate and CI
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #15
- prior: sprint-16 security-audit

## Problem / gap

`cargo audit` is not installed or invoked in CI. Wasmtime v29→v33 closed 10 transitive Dependabot alerts, but there is no automated gate to catch the next round.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `cargo audit` installed in CI image.
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh gate` invokes `cargo audit` (with allow-list for known-accepted advisories).
- [ ] CI fails on new advisories.
- [ ] Documented in `docs/handoff.md` / build-script docs.

## Dependencies

- blocks: 0.7.0 release confidence
- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- XS effort; pure tooling. Should land before publish.

name: ws02: mesh — implement K6.4 chain replay (LocalChain::tail_from + append_signed)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #16
- code: `crates/clawft-kernel/src/mesh_runtime.rs:509-535` (chain-sync stub)
- prior: k6-readiness-audit

## Problem / gap

K6.4 chain replay is half-built: `build_chain_sync_request` and `handle_chain_sync_response` exist, but no replay into `ChainManager`. Cross-node chain sync is therefore non-functional.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `LocalChain::tail_from(seq)` returns events newer than seq.
- [ ] Remote events apply via `ChainManager::append_signed()`.
- [ ] Conflict / merge policy decided (links to row #20 Q1).
- [ ] Two-node integration test shows convergence.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: row #20 (chain merge strategy decision), row #23 (test harness)

## Notes

- L effort; foundational to any K6 mesh story.

name: ws02: mesh — implement K6.4 tree Merkle diff + signed mutations
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #17
- prior: k6-readiness-audit Tree RED

## Problem / gap

Resource tree has no Merkle proof generation, no diff API, and `MutationEvent.signature` is always `None`. Cross-node tree sync cannot be authenticated or transmitted incrementally.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Merkle diff API on `ResourceTree` exposes minimal-witness deltas.
- [ ] `MutationEvent` carries Ed25519 signatures end-to-end.
- [ ] Two-node tree-diff integration test.
- [ ] Performance: diff is O(changed) not O(tree).

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: row #56 (incremental Merkle hash)
- pairs-with: row #55 (MutationEvent signing)

## Notes

- L effort; security-critical for any multi-node deployment.

name: ws02: mesh — implement S10 key-rotation chain event + verifier
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #18
- prior: k6-decision-coverage S10

## Problem / gap

S10 key rotation has no owner in the K6 plan. Without it, a compromised node key requires cluster-wide manual intervention. The security panel defines a 5-step dual-signed-chain-event protocol but nothing implements it.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Dual-signed chain event for key rotation per security-panel spec.
- [ ] Verifier rejects events not signed by both old and new keys during the rotation window.
- [ ] CLI: `weaver kernel rotate-key`.
- [ ] Tests for happy path + replay rejection.

## Dependencies

- blocks: any production multi-node deployment
- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- M effort; security-critical for K6 operations.

name: ws02: mesh — implement IpcScope::Restricted browser default + browser_policy rules
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #19
- prior: k6-decision-coverage S7

## Problem / gap

S7 (browser node restrictions) has only partial coverage. No exit criterion tests `IpcScope::Restricted` as the browser default, and no governance `browser_policy` rules exist. Browser nodes can therefore reach IPC scopes they should not.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Browser builds default to `IpcScope::Restricted`.
- [ ] `browser_policy` rule type lands in governance.
- [ ] Test: browser node cannot subscribe to non-public topics.
- [ ] Documented in `docs/guides/browser.md`.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- S effort; pairs with FS/env persistence work in ws01.

name: ws02: mesh — decide chain merge strategy (leader vs DAG) and split-brain handling
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #20
- prior: k6-developer-readiness Q1, Q5

## Problem / gap

Q1 (chain merge: leader vs DAG) and Q5 (split-brain handling) are open design questions blocking K6.4 implementation. Without a decision, chain replay (row #16) cannot land cleanly.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ADR added documenting the chosen merge strategy.
- [ ] Split-brain detection + recovery semantics defined.
- [ ] Decision referenced by row #16 implementation.

## Dependencies

- blocks: row #16 (chain replay)

## Notes

- M effort; design + ADR work, no code per se.

name: ws02: mesh — decide and freeze KernelMessage wire format (JSON vs RVF)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #21
- prior: k6-developer-readiness Q2

## Problem / gap

Q2 wire format is open. Pseudocode uses `serialize_rvf()` but `KernelMessage` is currently JSON on the wire. Need a freeze before K6 ships.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ADR documents chosen wire format.
- [ ] `KernelMessage` serialization aligned with ADR.
- [ ] Cross-language compatibility considerations recorded.
- [ ] Test pins the format.

## Dependencies

- pairs-with: rows #16, #17 (sync paths consume this)

## Notes

- S effort; mostly decision + small implementation alignment.

name: ws02: mesh — decide full libp2p-kad vs lighter DHT
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #22
- prior: k6-developer-readiness Q4

## Problem / gap

Q4 is unresolved: stick with full libp2p-kad or build a lighter DHT? This is research / experimentation territory and depends on field telemetry once K6 ships at all.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ADR documenting decision and rationale.
- [ ] If lighter DHT: design doc + spike code.
- [ ] Decision aligned with ADR-027 (selective libp2p).

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: K6 mesh ships somewhere first to gather data

## Notes

- Speculative / research item; safe to defer to 0.9.x.

name: ws02: mesh — add InMemoryTransport / MockPeer / MockClock / FaultyTransport
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #23
- prior: k6-test-strategy-review P0

## Problem / gap

No multi-node integration test harness exists. K6 mesh code is ~3,500 lines with 133 unit tests but zero two-node integration tests. The recommended fixtures (`InMemoryTransport`, `MockPeer`, `MockClock`, `FaultyTransport`) belong in `crates/clawft-kernel/src/mesh_test_support.rs`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `mesh_test_support.rs` lands with the four fixtures.
- [ ] At least one two-node integration test exercises each fixture.
- [ ] Fixtures documented for downstream test authors.

## Dependencies

- blocks: rows #16, #17, #18 (sync work needs this harness)

## Notes

- M effort; foundational for any K6 testability story.

name: ws02: mesh — define Clock trait and inject into time-dependent components
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #24
- prior: k6-test-strategy-review P0

## Problem / gap

Mesh time-dependent components (heartbeat, SWIM, retries) hardwire `std::time::Instant` / `tokio::time`, blocking deterministic tests. Need a `Clock` trait so `MockClock` (row #23) can drive them.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `Clock` trait in `clawft-kernel`.
- [ ] All time-dependent mesh components consume `Clock` via DI.
- [ ] Production binds to a `RealClock`.
- [ ] Test using `MockClock` deterministically advances heartbeat ticks.

## Dependencies

- pairs-with: row #23 (MockClock fixture)

## Notes

- M effort; large refactor surface but mechanical.

name: ws02: ci — add cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown (no mesh) to CI
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #25
- prior: k6-test-strategy-review P0

## Problem / gap

No CI gate ensures `clawft-kernel` (mesh feature off) builds for `wasm32-unknown-unknown`. Browser builds risk silent breakage when non-browser code creeps into the default feature set.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] CI runs `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --no-default-features` (or feature-equivalent).
- [ ] Failure blocks merge.
- [ ] Documented in `scripts/build.sh`.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- XS effort; pure CI plumbing.

name: ws02: mesh — define missing K6 protocol struct types and msg_type enum
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #26
- prior: k6-developer-readiness

## Problem / gap

Multiple protocol-message struct types are referenced but undefined: `MeshStream`, `TransportListener`, `EncryptedPeer`, `WeftHandshake`, `JoinRequest/Response`, `TreeSyncRequest/Response`, `ServiceEndpoint`, `ProcessAdvertisement`, `ServiceAdvertisement`, `Frame`, plus the full `msg_type` enumeration. Implementation cannot proceed without them.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Each type lands as a concrete struct/enum with serde derives.
- [ ] `msg_type` enum exhaustively covers wire variants.
- [ ] Roundtrip serialization tests for each.
- [ ] ADR / mesh-protocol doc updated to reference the shipped types.

## Dependencies

- blocks: rows #16, #17, downstream sync paths
- blocked-by: row #21 (wire format decision)

## Notes

- M effort; mostly type plumbing once row #21 is decided.

name: ws02: mesh — resolve mesh_adapter.rs vs mesh_ipc.rs and mesh/handshake.rs layout
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #27
- prior: k6-developer-readiness

## Problem / gap

Documentation references `mesh_adapter.rs` in the K6.3 phase breakdown but it is not in the "Files to Create" table; meanwhile `mesh/handshake.rs` is a sub-directory while every other mesh file uses flat `mesh_*.rs`. Either the layout standard is wrong or the file isn't where the docs say.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Layout standard documented (flat vs nested).
- [ ] Files moved or doc updated to match.
- [ ] No dangling cross-references.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- XS; cleanup work.

name: ws02: mesh — wire AssessmentTransport into daemon + add weft assess mesh-status
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #28
- prior: mesh-k6-transport Next Steps

## Problem / gap

`AssessmentTransport` is fully tested but never wired into the daemon mesh event loop. Assessment diff propagation (push only changed findings) does not happen at runtime. No CLI exposes mesh-assess state.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `AssessmentTransport` registered in the daemon mesh event loop.
- [ ] `weft assess mesh-status` CLI subcommand reports peer state.
- [ ] Assessment diff propagation pushes only changed findings.
- [ ] Integration test covers two-node propagation.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: row #23 (test harness)

## Notes

- M effort; orphan code that needs an owner.

name: ws02: mesh — add QUIC transport (quinn + snow) alongside TCP/WS
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #29
- prior: mesh-boot-integration Future Work; ADR-026

## Problem / gap

ADR-026 makes QUIC the primary transport, but only TCP (and WS) are implemented. Without QUIC, mesh suffers head-of-line blocking and worse NAT traversal.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] QUIC transport via `quinn` + `snow` Noise.
- [ ] Selectable via `[kernel.mesh]` config.
- [ ] Two-node integration test over QUIC.
- [ ] Documented in `docs/guides/`.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: row #23 (test harness)

## Notes

- L effort; ADR-026 commitment.

name: ws02: mesh — make Mesh a SystemService with start/stop/health_check
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #30
- prior: mesh-boot-integration Future Work

## Problem / gap

Mesh boots in phase 5d but is not a `SystemService` — it has no proper start/stop/health_check lifecycle. Operators cannot inspect or restart it via the standard service surface.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `MeshService` implements `SystemService`.
- [ ] Start/stop/health_check exposed via `weaver kernel services`.
- [ ] Tests for lifecycle transitions.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- S effort; routine service-ification.

name: ws02: mesh — wire ClusterService to mesh peer discovery
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #31
- prior: mesh-boot-integration Future Work

## Problem / gap

`ClusterService` is pull-based today. Mesh peer discovery exists separately. Linking the two would let cluster membership reflect live mesh state without manual sync.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ClusterService subscribes to mesh peer-event stream.
- [ ] Membership updates atomically.
- [ ] Tests for join / leave / partition recovery.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: row #23 (test harness)

## Notes

- M effort; integration glue.

name: ws02: mesh — implement mesh time-sync (authority election, offset smoothing, mesh_time)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #32
- prior: `.planning/development_notes/mesh-time-sync.md` (design only)

## Problem / gap

Mesh time sync is design-only: no `authority_time` / `authority_id` fields, no election (GPS > NTP > local), no offset smoothing, no `MeshRuntime::mesh_time()` API, no WiFi TSF integration. Required by ECC causal-edge timestamps, ExoChain `chain_seq` monotonicity across nodes, topology browser temporal dim, robotics sensor fusion.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `authority_time` / `authority_id` heartbeat fields.
- [ ] Authority election (GPS > NTP > local monotonic).
- [ ] EMA offset smoothing + outlier rejection.
- [ ] `MeshRuntime::mesh_time()` API.
- [ ] WiFi TSF integration for ESP32 (or flagged out).

## Dependencies

- blocks: cross-node ECC, ExoChain monotonicity
- blocked-by: row #23 (test harness with MockClock)

## Notes

- L effort; orphaned design doc with no current owner.

name: ws02: services-api — wire axum handlers to http_facade types + SSE loop
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #33
- prior: http-facade Next Steps

## Problem / gap

`http_facade.rs` defines 13 routes + SSE + witness, but no axum handler in `clawft-services/src/api/` calls into it. The facade is orphaned. SSE streaming loop also missing.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] axum handlers in `clawft-services/src/api/` call into `http_facade` types.
- [ ] SSE handler loops on `poll_events()`.
- [ ] Smoke test for each route + an SSE client smoke test.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: rows #10, #11, #12 (auth/CORS/rate-limit) for production exposure

## Notes

- M effort; routine wiring once safety stack is in place.

name: ws02: services-api — add HTTP facade integration tests once profile/pairing types land
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #34
- prior: http-facade Next Steps

## Problem / gap

HTTP facade integration tests are blocked on `ProfilesConfig` / `PairingConfig` types landing in `clawft-types`. Once those land, the integration coverage should follow.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `ProfilesConfig` / `PairingConfig` available.
- [ ] Integration tests cover the 13 routes + SSE.
- [ ] CI runs the suite.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: types landing

## Notes

- S effort once the prerequisites land.

name: ws02: vector — wire VectorBackend into DemocritusLoop
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #35
- prior: vector-hybrid Next steps

## Problem / gap

DEMOCRITUS still consumes a raw `HnswService`. The new `VectorBackend` trait + Hybrid backend exists but is unused on the cognitive path. Switching unlocks DiskANN / Hybrid swap-in.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `DemocritusLoop` consumes `Arc<dyn VectorBackend>`.
- [ ] Default binding is the existing HNSW backend (no behavior change).
- [ ] Tests confirm backend swap is wiring-only.

## Dependencies

- blocks: row #37 (DiskANN) when upstream lands

## Notes

- S effort.

name: ws02: vector — add ecc.vector-config RPC endpoint
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #36
- prior: vector-hybrid Next steps

## Problem / gap

No RPC exposes the active vector backend, so operators have no introspection. A small `ecc.vector-config` RPC closes that gap.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `ecc.vector-config` RPC returns active backend + parameters.
- [ ] `weaver kernel ecc vector-config` CLI consumes it.
- [ ] Test for RPC contract.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: row #35 (VectorBackend wired)

## Notes

- XS effort.

name: ws02: vector — ship real DiskANN backend behind diskann feature once ruvector-diskann publishes
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #37
- code: `crates/clawft-kernel/src/vector_diskann.rs` (brute-force HashMap stub); `boot.rs:1415` log says "DiskANN (stub)"
- prior: vector-hybrid Next steps

## Problem / gap

DiskANN backend is a brute-force `HashMap<u64, StoredEntry>` with linear cosine search awaiting `ruvector-diskann` publication. Speculative / blocked-on-upstream.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `ruvector-diskann` published.
- [ ] Real DiskANN backend behind `diskann` feature flag.
- [ ] Boot log no longer says "DiskANN (stub)".
- [ ] Performance benchmark vs HNSW recorded.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: ruvector-diskann publication

## Notes

- M effort once upstream ships; pure waiting otherwise.

name: ws02: vector — persist HNSW tombstones across save/load
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #38
- prior: vector-hardening (Tombstones note)

## Problem / gap

HNSW tombstones are in-memory only. After a save/load cycle, soft-deleted vectors come back. Persistence requires extending the save/load format.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Save/load format persists tombstone state.
- [ ] Backward-compatible read for existing snapshots.
- [ ] Test: delete -> save -> load -> deleted entries stay gone.
- [ ] Compaction story documented.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- M effort; deferred to vector sync work in WS4 / Gap #11 per planning notes.

name: ws02: vector — flip LogQuantized + SimdDistance is_available once ruvector-core PR #352 lands
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #39
- code: `crates/clawft-kernel/src/vector_quantization.rs:83` (KG-011), `:150` (KG-012)
- prior: ruvector-core PR #352 (upstream)

## Problem / gap

`LogQuantizedConfig::is_available()` and `SimdDistanceConfig::is_available()` are hardwired `false` pending ruvector-core PR #352. Once that lands, flip the constants and the +14% QPS branch-free distance path opens.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ruvector-core PR #352 merged + released.
- [ ] Flags flipped, code paths exercised.
- [ ] Benchmark shows the +14% QPS lift.
- [ ] TODO(KG-011/KG-012) comments removed.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: ruvector-core PR #352

## Notes

- XS once upstream ships.

name: ws02: kernel — ship real Wasmtime backend for spectral_embedding (or move to deferred)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #40
- code: `crates/clawft-kernel/src/quantum_register.rs:9` (TODO)

## Problem / gap

`spectral_embedding` is a TODO. Either ship a real Wasmtime backend or move it to the deferred-feature appendix and remove from the production surface.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Decision recorded: ship vs defer.
- [ ] If ship: real backend with tests.
- [ ] If defer: feature-flagged out and documented.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- M effort if shipping; XS if deferring.

name: ws02: exo-resource-tree — replace permission.rs always-Allow stub with K1 ACL engine
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #41
- code: `crates/exo-resource-tree/src/permission.rs:23-31` (explicit K0 stub: always returns `Allow`)
- prior: exo-resource-tree K1

## Problem / gap

`permission.rs` is a deliberate K0 stub that always returns `Allow`. Resource-tree access is therefore unauthenticated at the permission layer. K1 deliverable: ACL-based permission checks with delegation shortcut, `EffectiveAclCache`, integration with `CapabilityChecker`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ACL engine implemented; default-deny fall-through.
- [ ] `EffectiveAclCache` lands.
- [ ] `CapabilityChecker` integrated.
- [ ] Negative tests confirm denial paths.
- [ ] `weaver kernel acl` CLI surface for inspection.

## Dependencies

- blocks: 0.7.0 governance audit
- blocked-by: none (the stub is already the holdup)

## Notes

- L effort; treated as 0.7.x release-gate-blocker because the audit explicitly cites it as a kernel governance gap.

name: ws02: exo-resource-tree — implement DelegationCert lifecycle (grant/revoke + Ed25519 + expiry)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #42
- code: `crates/exo-resource-tree/src/delegation.rs:14` (type only, no grant/revoke/sig/expiry/chain-validate)
- prior: exo-resource-tree K1

## Problem / gap

`DelegationCert` is a type stub with no lifecycle. K1 deliverables: grant/revoke with Ed25519 signatures, certificate chain validation, time-bounded expiry.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Grant/revoke APIs with Ed25519 signing.
- [ ] Chain validation against trust anchor.
- [ ] Expiry honored.
- [ ] Integration test with multi-cert chain.

## Dependencies

- pairs-with: row #41 (ACL engine consumes delegations)

## Notes

- M effort; foundational for delegation-aware governance.

name: ws02: services-api — implement bridge.rs TODOs (skill, memory, config)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #43
- code: `crates/clawft-services/src/api/bridge.rs:282, 287, 395, 467` (unimplemented bridge endpoints)

## Problem / gap

Four bridge endpoints are unimplemented: skill install/uninstall, memory delete, config persistence. UI features that depend on them silently fail.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Each endpoint implemented with proper error mapping.
- [ ] Tests cover happy + error paths.
- [ ] Documented in API reference.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- M effort; routine.

name: ws02: services-api — add CSP middleware to API tower stack
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #44
- code: `crates/clawft-services/src/api/handlers.rs:130` (TODO CSP middleware)

## Problem / gap

No Content-Security-Policy headers on API responses. UI is therefore exposed to XSS / inline-script abuse.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] CSP middleware attached to the tower stack.
- [ ] Sensible default policy that doesn't break the UI.
- [ ] Configurable via kernel config.
- [ ] Test asserts header presence.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- XS effort.

name: ws02: tests — resolve test-suite hang in clawft-kernel --lib aggregate run
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #45
- prior: CHANGELOG 0.6.19 Known Issues

## Problem / gap

When the full `clawft-kernel --lib` test suite is run, one test hangs. Targeted runs pass. This blocks reliable CI and any pre-commit gate that runs the full suite.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Hang reproduced in a controlled harness.
- [ ] Root cause identified (likely an unbounded `select!`, missing timeout, or resource leak).
- [ ] Fix landed; full `--lib` run passes deterministically.
- [ ] CI gate reinstated.

## Dependencies

- blocks: 0.7.0 publish (CI confidence)
- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- M effort; treated as release-gate-blocker because reliable CI is a 0.7.0 prerequisite.

name: ws02: workspace — clean ~150 clippy errors (pre-existing debt)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #46
- prior: CHANGELOG 0.6.19 Known Issues

## Problem / gap

`scripts/build.sh check` is green but `scripts/build.sh clippy` is red on ~150 pre-existing errors across `clawft-types/src/goal.rs`, `clawft-rpc`, `eml-core`, and older kernel/weave code. Blocks any clippy-as-gate aspiration.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Errors triaged: trivial, mechanical, structural.
- [ ] Mechanical batch fixed.
- [ ] Remaining structural issues filed as smaller follow-ups.
- [ ] `scripts/build.sh clippy` green.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- M effort; sequence after 0.7.0 to avoid distracting churn.

name: ws02: kernel — persist AppManager state to disk
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #47
- prior: k0-k5-final-gap-analysis §6

## Problem / gap

Apps installed during a daemon session are lost on restart. AppManager has no on-disk manifest store.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Manifest store persists installed apps.
- [ ] Boot rehydrates AppManager from disk.
- [ ] Atomic write (tmp + rename) to avoid corruption.
- [ ] Test: install -> restart -> app still present.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- S effort; mirrors cluster_peers persistence pattern.

name: ws02: chain — implement chain-anchored anchoring beyond MockAnchor
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #48
- code: `crates/clawft-kernel/src/chain.rs:2180-2212` (`ChainAnchor` trait + `MockAnchor`)
- prior: ADR-041 (chainanchor-trait)

## Problem / gap

The `ChainAnchor` trait has only a `MockAnchor` impl. No external-ledger anchoring is wired up. Speculative until a target ledger is chosen.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Concrete anchor against an external ledger (e.g. Bitcoin OP_RETURN, Ethereum log, custom hash chain).
- [ ] Configurable via kernel config.
- [ ] Anchoring frequency policy.
- [ ] Tests with mock ledger.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: ledger-target decision

## Notes

- M effort; speculative, deferred to 0.9.x.

name: ws02: docs — update docs/weftos/k-phases.md (K2.1/K3/K4/K5 mis-marked)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #49
- prior: k0-k5-final §1, §3

## Problem / gap

`docs/weftos/k-phases.md` shows K2.1 as PENDING and K3/K4/K5 as STUBBED when all are complete. Misleading public docs at 0.7.0 cut.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] K-phase status table reflects shipped state.
- [ ] Cross-checked against `k0-k5-final-gap-analysis.md`.
- [ ] Build green for the docs site.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- XS effort; ship before 0.7.0 to avoid embarrassment in public docs.

name: ws02: docs — write docs/guides/kernel.md (deferred from K5)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #50
- prior: k0-k5-final

## Problem / gap

K5 deliverable `docs/guides/kernel.md` does not exist. Operators have no curated entry point for kernel concepts beyond the architecture doc.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `docs/guides/kernel.md` lands covering boot phases, services, K-phases, governance, ExoChain at an operator level.
- [ ] Linked from the docs index.
- [ ] Reviewed for accuracy against current code.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- M effort; can ship after 0.7.0 since architecture doc covers the worst gap.

name: ws02: docs — renumber duplicate ADRs (two ADR-020s, two ADR-028s)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #51 + Open questions
- files: ADR-020 (chainloggable + kernel-phase-responsibilities); ADR-028 (post-quantum-dual-signing in `docs/adr/` + weftos-kernel in `docs/architecture/`)

## Problem / gap

Two ADRs share number 020 and two share number 028. Tooling and humans both confuse them. Trivial cleanup that should ship before the public 0.7.0 doc release.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Each ADR carries a unique number.
- [ ] `docs/architecture/` vs `docs/adr/` paths consolidated to one canonical location.
- [ ] Cross-references updated.
- [ ] ADR index regenerated.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- XS effort; release-gate because doc release is part of 0.7.0 and these duplicates would ship publicly otherwise.

name: ws02: docs — accept ADR-023 (assessment-as-kernel-service)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #52
- file: `adr-023-assessment-as-kernel-service` (status: Proposed)

## Problem / gap

ADR-023 is still 'Proposed' even though the mesh-assess code that depends on it has shipped. Either accept the ADR or mark dependent code provisional.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ADR status flipped to Accepted (or dependent code annotated provisional).
- [ ] Decision rationale appended if any.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- XS effort; pure paperwork.

name: ws02: kernel — add NodeId composite for cross-node uniqueness + remote inbox bridge
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #53
- prior: k6-readiness-audit

## Problem / gap

`Pid: u64` has no node component, so PIDs collide across nodes. `Inbox` mpsc is in-process only; remote delivery has no bridge. Identity / addressing model is incomplete for any multi-node story.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `(NodeId, Pid)` composite identity used at IPC boundaries.
- [ ] Remote inbox bridge proxies messages across mesh.
- [ ] Tests for cross-node send / receive.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: row #23 (test harness)

## Notes

- L effort; foundational for K6.

name: ws02: kernel — enforce max-message-size on KernelIpc::send (16 MiB)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #54
- prior: k6-readiness-audit Security RED

## Problem / gap

Max-message-size is enforced in `mesh_framing` (16 MiB) but not on raw `KernelIpc::send` deserialization. A malicious local sender can OOM the daemon by submitting an oversized message via the IPC fast path.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `KernelIpc::send` rejects messages > 16 MiB pre-deserialization.
- [ ] Configurable cap (default 16 MiB).
- [ ] Test with oversized payload.

## Dependencies

- blocks: 0.7.0 publish (security gap)
- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- S effort; high-leverage security fix.

name: ws02: mesh — add MutationEvent Ed25519 signing for cross-node tree mutations
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #55
- prior: k6-readiness-audit Tree RED

## Problem / gap

`MutationEvent.signature` is always `None`. Cross-node tree mutations cannot be authenticated. Pairs with row #17 (tree Merkle diff).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `MutationEvent` produced with Ed25519 signature.
- [ ] Receiving node verifies signature.
- [ ] Tests for valid + tampered + replay cases.

## Dependencies

- pairs-with: row #17 (tree Merkle diff)

## Notes

- M effort.

name: ws02: mesh — incremental Merkle hash updates (replace full recompute_all)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #56
- prior: k6-readiness-audit Tree YELLOW

## Problem / gap

`ResourceTree::recompute_all()` is a full-tree rehash on every mutation; no incremental dirty-flag propagation. O(tree) work per mutation makes large trees painful.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Dirty-flag propagation up the tree.
- [ ] Recompute scoped to affected subtree.
- [ ] Benchmark: large tree mutation is O(depth) not O(size).
- [ ] Tests verify hash equivalence with the old path.

## Dependencies

- pairs-with: row #17 (Merkle diff API)

## Notes

- M effort; pure perf.

name: ws02: mesh — replace static GovernanceRule Vec with cluster-wide distribution
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #57
- prior: k6-readiness-audit Governance RED

## Problem / gap

`Vec<GovernanceRule>` is static and per-node. Cross-node governance requires rule distribution + escalation, otherwise nodes drift on policy.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Cluster-wide rule distribution mechanism.
- [ ] Cross-node escalation when local policy denies.
- [ ] Versioned rules with conflict resolution.
- [ ] Tests for split, merge, and escalation.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: row #16 (chain replay), row #20 (merge strategy)

## Notes

- L effort; deeply coupled to K6 chain story.

name: ws02: mesh — cross-node capability-claim verification (signed advertisement)
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #58
- prior: k6-readiness-audit Security RED

## Problem / gap

`PeerNode.capabilities` strings are unvalidated. Any peer can advertise any capability. Cross-node capability advertisement must be signed and verifiable.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Capability advertisements carry Ed25519 signatures.
- [ ] Receiving nodes verify.
- [ ] Capability strings validated against an allow-list.
- [ ] Tests for valid + tampered.

## Dependencies

- pairs-with: row #55 (signing infra)

## Notes

- M effort.

name: ws02: mesh — rate-limit add_peer() and governance-evaluation requests
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #59
- prior: k6-readiness-audit Security RED

## Problem / gap

No DoS protection on `add_peer()` or governance evaluation. A malicious or buggy peer can flood either path.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Rate limit on `add_peer()` per source.
- [ ] Rate limit on governance-evaluation per principal.
- [ ] Configurable thresholds.
- [ ] Tests for limit enforcement.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- S effort.

name: ws02: docs — document DEMOCRITUS 'still stuck' log-line semantics
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #60 + Open questions
- code: `crates/clawft-kernel/src/cognitive_tick.rs:472`

## Problem / gap

The DEMOCRITUS "still stuck after N checks" warning is expected behavior on empty causal graphs and idle conversations, not a bug. v0.6.19 backoff keeps it quiet but operators will still see it. Without docs, every new operator will file a false-positive bug.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Operator-facing docs explain the log line, when it's normal, when it's worth investigating.
- [ ] Linked from troubleshooting guide.
- [ ] Cross-referenced from kernel guide (row #50).

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- XS effort; release-gate because operator confusion at the 0.7.0 cut would be expensive.

name: ws02: kernel — verify weftos-leaf-types push path goes through governance / chain
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #61
- prior: v0.6.17 leaf push CLI

## Problem / gap

Unclear whether `weftos-leaf-types` (v0.6.17 leaf push CLI) routes pushes through governance and ExoChain. No workstream claims it.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Audit the leaf-push code path end-to-end.
- [ ] Either confirm governance + chain coverage or document the bypass.
- [ ] Add gates if missing.
- [ ] Test asserts the push event lands in the chain.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- S effort; orphan triage.

name: ws02: mesh — audit mesh_log/mesh_dedup/mesh_listener/mesh_bootstrap for callers
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #62

## Problem / gap

`mesh_log`, `mesh_dedup`, `mesh_listener`, `mesh_bootstrap` are implemented but have no daemon RPC / CLI / observability surface. Either they have a hidden caller, or they're orphans.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] End-to-end caller traced for each module.
- [ ] If wired: documented and tested.
- [ ] If orphaned: scheduled for wiring or marked deprecated.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- S effort; triage work.

name: ws02: tests — confirm cognitum-gate-tilezero Permit/Defer/Deny path is exercised
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #63

## Problem / gap

`cognitum-gate-tilezero` (TileZeroGate) Permit/Defer/Deny three-way cryptographic-receipt path has no documented tests. May or may not be exercised in production.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Audit existing test coverage.
- [ ] Add tests for any missing branch.
- [ ] Document the receipt format.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- S effort.

name: ws02: chain — add EVENT_KIND_* constants for minor non-kernel chain gaps
description_md:
## Source

- audit: `.planning/reviews/0.7.0-release-gate/02-kernel-governance.md` Task List row #64
- prior: exochain-certification-nonkernel Minor Gaps

## Problem / gap

Four minor non-kernel sites do not emit chain events: `ToolRegistry::register_with_metadata`, `sandbox::check_tool|network|file_read|file_write` (only audit-log), and `clawft-graphify::ingest::save_query_result`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `EVENT_KIND_*` constants defined for each.
- [ ] Each call site emits the correct event.
- [ ] Tests confirm events reach the chain.

## Dependencies

- blocked-by: none

## Notes

- S effort; finishes the non-kernel chain story.
